rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities before assert
Known correct Claude Code facts — transcript path format
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body (line 30) uses '<encoded>' which is correct. Maintainer artefact table uses '<project-folder>' (imprecise vs canonical '<encoded-cwd>') but lives below <!-- maintainer --> and does not reach students.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer-block imprecision only; student-visible line 30 is correct. Low-priority cleanup.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Transcript-by-mtime selection fragile; marker-based selection required
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module body correctly references the M4 session transcript via fallback to 'SHA Claude reported at Module 4 close' — marker-based, not mtime-only. Artefact contract table documents <session-id>.jsonl correctly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-working-directory boundaries always open new
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module correctly instructs 'Open a new Claude Code session in the worktree' after forking — not 'return' or 'resume'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — open a new session, not open Claude Code
Source URL + type label mandatory; maintainer-block scope
rules_evaluated.evidence: Source-verification maintainer block present. Prework URLs include Entis/Klaassen (every.to) with correct attribution pattern: 'Laura Entis on Kieran Klaassen' (practitioner analysis). Klaassen's own articles cited as practitioner direct. Labels live in maintainer block only; body uses builder voice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Freshness 6 months
rules_evaluated.evidence: Source-verification block includes 'freshness re-check before first cohort' instructions. All three every.to URLs confirmed alive 2026-05-14.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Zombie stat guard
rules_evaluated.evidence: No round-number AI failure/success statistics in student-facing body. Specific number '19.2% auto-approved' (Curran/Intercom) lives in maintainer source-verification block with explicit verify-before-cohort instruction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block on student-facing files with practitioner citations
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block contains '**Source verification — MUST DO before first cohort**' section listing every URL, specific numbers to triple-check (19.2%), fallback framings, and freshness re-check instructions.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: Angle-bracket templates (<repo-name>, <slug>) appear in ae101-m5-worktree-setup prompt body. Rule 1 bans [BRACKETS] square-bracket placeholders only; angle-bracket templates are derive-from-context instructions Claude resolves at runtime. See compounded/2026-05-03-platform-technical-judge-fp-square-vs-angle-and-scrollback-chain.md — known FP shape, marked PASS per calibration.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both prompt markers have action lead-ins before them in the module body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path, not invented alias
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m5-rerun-packaged uses 'the paths we wrote in this session' — a scrollback-review chain, which rule 5 explicitly PREFERS. Not a deterministic-path violation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Preamble before action — structural trigger at 4+ sub-steps requires suppressor or body primer
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m5-worktree-setup (prompt-001, expanded at line 28) has 6 named sub-steps: find branch, validate log, spin worktree, cp CLAUDE.local.md, mkdir .claude/, cp memory/. No in-fence 'No preamble.' suppressor at end of registry body. No body primer immediately after the marker naming the preamble + skim-past cue. The body text after the marker is a multi-result fallback callout (rule 33), not a preamble primer.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add body primer immediately after {{prompt:ae101-m5-worktree-setup}}: 'Claude will narrate before acting; skip past the opening and look for the worktree path and file-copy confirmation.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in registry; module files reference by marker
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both prompts use {{prompt:ae101-m5-worktree-setup}} and {{prompt:ae101-m5-rerun-packaged}} markers. Registry files exist at curriculum/prompts/ with correct frontmatter (key, dest, runtime, origin).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No module-name references in prompt prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m5-worktree-setup prompt body (prompt-001) line 9: 'so M5/M6 compounding has the M1/M3 evidence to build on' — four bare M-number references (M5, M6, M1, M3) in prose, not inside backtick code spans, double-quotes, or path segments. Also 'compounding' is flagged by the P4 rule in prompt-source-audit.sh. Exemptions (branch names in code spans, commit messages in double-quotes) do not apply to this prose line.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rewrite prose line to reference artefacts not module numbers: e.g. 'so the verifier-build and re-send have the personal-rules and memory evidence from earlier sessions to build on'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Git-output greps that assert exact-N need multi-result fallback
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m5-worktree-setup prompt-001 includes 'There may be more than one' inside the fence AND the body immediately after the marker has a bolded callout 'The grep may hit multiple branches.' with specific fallback instructions (log-grep + SHA from transcript). Rule 33 option A correctly implemented.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed skills invoke by name, never by path
rules_evaluated.evidence: No skill invocations inside fenced prompt blocks. 'Ralph re-feed' is a concept/shape name in LOs and leap test (body text), not a [Skill: ralph-loop] invocation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism — named tools, skills, files must exist and be accessible
rules_evaluated.evidence: All referenced exercises and lectures exist: diagnose-and-resend.md (curriculum/exercises/), reading-the-return.md, learning-through-contrast.md, what-packaging-is.md (curriculum/lectures/). Registry prompts exist. No named skills invoked in prompts.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase-level forcing functions live inside the exercise file (diagnose-and-resend.md). The two prompts in this module file (worktree-setup, rerun-packaged) encode their own forcing functions inside their fenced blocks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs smoke-test before trusted
rules_evaluated.evidence: Verifier-build is in the exercise file (Phase 3 of diagnose-and-resend.md). The re-send prompt chains on the built verifier after it is constructed and tested in Phase 3. Per-phase failure-mode table names the verifier-shape mismatch failure and escape hatch.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers as staged progression
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module correctly treats CLAUDE.local.md and .claude/memory/ as explicit-load artefacts requiring explicit copy at worktree fork. No auto-load assumptions made about personal files crossing worktree boundaries.

platform_claims_checked.claim: gitignored files (CLAUDE.local.md, .claude/memory/) don't ride into a worktree because git doesn't see them
Cross-working-directory boundary (worktree) opens new session — 'Open a new Claude Code session in the worktree'
M5 session transcript at ~/.claude/projects/<project-folder>/<session-id>.jsonl (maintainer artefact table)
platform_claims_checked.note: Uses '<project-folder>' instead of canonical '<encoded-cwd>'; imprecise but maintainer-block-only. Student-visible reference (line 30) uses '<encoded>' correctly.

citations_checked.claim: Laura Entis on Kieran Klaassen, 'You're the Bread in the AI Sandwich' (every.to)
Kieran Klaassen, 'Compound Engineering: The Definitive Guide' (every.to)
Kieran Klaassen, 'My AI Had Already Fixed the Code Before I Saw It' (every.to)
